pattern: A layered breadth-first search records every parent that first reaches each word at its shortest distance, then a backtracking walk over that predecessor graph enumerates all minimum-length transformation sequences.
hints: Only paths of the minimum possible length count, so explore level by level and stop expanding once the end word's level is reached.
During the level-order search, for each newly reached word remember all words on the previous level that reach it; these predecessor links form a graph of all shortest connections.

BFS from beginWord one layer at a time, recording for every word the set of immediate predecessors discovered on the layer that first reaches it; once endWord appears, backtrack from endWord through predecessors to assemble every shortest sequence.

edge_inputs.description: two shortest sequences sharing prefix and suffix
end word missing from the list yields no sequence
begin and end one apart, end present
two letters with two distinct shortest routes

stress.description: small constructed graph guaranteeing a reachable end with bounded path count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    letters = 'abcd'
    L = 4
    n = max(2, min(size, 14))
    begin = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(L))
    cur = list(begin)
    chain = []
    seen = {begin}
    for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 5)):
        pos = rng.randrange(L)
        old = cur[pos]
        cur[pos] = rng.choice([c for c in letters if c != old])
        w = ''.join(cur)
        if w not in seen:
            seen.add(w)
            chain.append(w)
    if not chain:
        chain = [begin[:-1] + ('a' if begin[-1] != 'a' else 'b')]
    end = chain[-1]
    wl = list(chain)
    for _ in range(n):
        w = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(L))
        if w not in seen:
            seen.add(w)
            wl.append(w)
    rng.shuffle(wl)
    return [begin, end, wl]
stress.note: constructed chain guarantees reachability; tiny alphabet and short length bound the number of shortest paths
